google_reviews:
Had the best time at Hawthorne and Wood on NYE!! TLDR: We feel like we discovered our new favorite restaurant on Wednesday.

To start off, our server (I cannot remember his name for the life of me but he had cool tattoos on his arms) was friendly, knowledgeable and just made sure drinks were always full and that the food was great. Never once felt forgotten about and he was great with recommendations/suggestions. For our first courses we ordered the Caviar which was one of the stars of the whole meal, it came with these seasoned crumpets and a hollandaise that meshed so well together we would’ve licked the bowl if we could’ve. We also got the focaccia bread which was PERFECT my only complaint for the full meal was that I didn’t love the orange in the orange rosemary butter but we opted out of adding butter to the bread and instead dipping it in the preserved lemon sauce from the New Orleans Barbecue Shrimp and let me tell you that was something from another world-addicting and delicious. Per the website, the chef has experience cooking in New Orleans and you can really tell with the insane flavors coming through in each bite. We followed this with the hamachi tiradito that was spectacular (almost like a ceviche, but the fish cooks in the aguachile) you have to make sure you get a bite of everything to see how those flavors come together. But one of the best parts of the meal was the pork shoulder appetizer that came with the polenta- this truly was to die for, we spoke about it so much the people next to us ordered the same and loved it. For the entree we chose to split the scallops and we almost wished we had ordered a full entree of it for ourselves instead because of how perfect the bacon jam and collards worked with the yam puree and allowed the scallops to shine (plus they were perfectly cooked). At this point of the meal we were stuffed and amazed but we knew we had to finish with a dessert and the sticky toffee pudding did not let us down one bit. One of the best desserts I have had in around here. It’s rare to have a restaurant blow your mind with every aspect of a meal and they really hit it out of the ballpark. This was a better meal than we have had at BIN54, ACME, Crawford Brothers, etc.. Just incredible food and we will definitely be back. 11/10. Serious compliments to the chef for the food and to all of the wait staff and front of house who made sure the holiday and quick pace of NYE didn’t affect their hospitality and kindness. ||| Everytime I come to Hawthorne & Wood the experience is great. Cozy ambiance. The food 10/10, well seasoned and cooked. I've sat at the bar multiple times, at a table and been served in the private room with a group. Great service everytime! This time I sat at the bar and ordered from the winter tasting menu with the wine paring. I highly recommend. Each dish was served as described and tasted great.  Nice parings too! My only issue was the steak been served a bit too rare for my liking. But I told my server and the cooked it a bit more and it was perfect. ||| Went to Hawthorne & Wood for my anniversary. The restaurant itself is very clean and beautiful. It definitely fits the casual fine dining vibe. Our server was very enthusiastic and provided good service.

The food was slightly disappointing for the price that we paid. Although it was good, the portions were very small and it's not like I was blown away by the taste. We got the Lamb Belly, Scallops, and Foie Gras. ||| My son brought me to Hawthorne & Wood for dinner, and it definitely lived up to everything I’ve heard. I’ve been wanting to try this spot for a long time, and I finally had my chance this week. I ordered the seared New Bedford scallops and my son had the sautéed flounder. We shared the gem salad and the roasted honeynut squash—every dish was outstanding.

The ambience is warm and inviting, the service was attentive, and the wine selection was excellent. A beautiful dining experience from start to finish. Highly recommend. ||| Hawthorne & wood was such a fun and fascinating experience. I hope to experience the quality and service here that I do at all the restaurants I visit. Truly an unforgettable experience. My boyfriend and I made the trip for my Mom’s 55th birthday and were delighted with the atmosphere. While we had no reservations and they were a little bit too busy for a table, we were invited to sit at the bar (which was quite a treat for us chatty folk!). My boyfriend and mom had the burger, which from their words was incredible. I, however, was curious about their tasting menu. For $70, I received a five course meal consisting of numerous seasonal and fascinating dishes. I would like to add that the lamb tartare blew me away! I was so fascinated with the spices and combinations of the ingredients that I would have never expected to work so excellently together! I have never had a restaurant nor a dish pick my brain in such a manner. The bartender who mainly worked in front of where we sat provided interesting input and answered all of our questions. We were also provided with a fascinating explanation on their cereal for dinner cocktail and how it is made! (High recommend asking, so interesting!) I had additionally mentioned to the bartender that it was my mom’s birthday, and we were so graciously gifted an additional chocolate truffle dessert, which, while small, was incredibly rich and delicious. Such a beautiful visit with incredible food, new flavors, and kind staff! Thank you Hawthorne & wood!

google_reviews:
I was the only car in the lot besides employees and nobody inside, I placed my order to go on the phone. I ordered house chips, French fries and the French dip.

The chips were crispy but had zero salt or flavor. The ranch was delicious.

The fries were so soggy I had to put them in the air fryer when I got home, again zero salt or flavor. Ate a few threw the rest.

My Philly was dry and flavorless, the au jus tasted like just beef stock. It should have flavor if it's been marinated with the meat properly well cooking. Ate a few bites and threw the rest.

I was hoping to like this place based on reviews, but if your someone who has a palette and likes to taste food this isn't the place for you. ||| My husband and I stopped here while we were passing through the area. I ordered the chicken shawarma and it was exactly what I expected. It was great! The girl taking out order was new and learning but very sweet. The others working with her were patient helping her. Also, the place was very clean! Overall, a great place and happy to have found it! ||| We had some great food at irock cafe tonight! I ordered the avacado Swiss burger. My burger was seasoned well, and had extremely fresh ingredients. My husband got the chicken shawarma, and the garlic sauce that came with it was amazing! We were dipping our fries in it. Reasonably priced too! The only thing I’d say is they only appeared to only have their menu on the wall, so if it’s difficult for you to make a decision while at the register, then you may want to decide first before walking up to the register. ||| I would like to express my utmost gratitude for the delightful experience my family and I had during our first visit to this restaurant. The ambiance and menu truly set this establishment apart in the most unique way. The staff members were exceptionally kind, especially considering the lively nature of my 10-month-old daughter. Their warm reception towards her made our dining experience even more enjoyable.

I must commend the impeccable service and reasonable prices that we encountered. I cannot find a single thing to fault about the restaurant or its staff. I must admit that I did not select an item directly from the menu; however, the staff graciously accommodated my request without any hesitation. My fiancé's choice of the French dip and various sandwiches left him satisfied, while the shrimp  surpassed all expectations and had me declaring it the best I have ever had.

The chicken was equally delightful, and overall, we were thoroughly impressed with the quality of the food. It is safe to say that we will be returning to this establishment in the near future. Thank you once again for a truly memorable dining experience.

I must humbly admit that there was a bit more food on my plate prior to taking the picture. However, the dish was so incredibly delicious that I had already consumed half of it before realizing the oversight. ||| I stopped in for the first time today and this place is so cute! The manager took my order and was so sweet and patient considering all the questions I asked him lol! I ordered the mozzarella sticks & chicken shawarma with the cream and potato soup. All of it was really really fresh & yummy! If you’re in the area you should definitely stop by, I’m excited to come back and try more!

google_reviews:
Way to go Greenville!!! This EC Pho is legit!!! Jonny added to the experience with his excellent customer service!!! My wife got the House Special Pho and it had so much meat in it that she had enough to spoil our dogs later! I tried the 5 Spice Chicken and it was delicious!!! The appetizers were delicious with the Fish Balls being good and fishy! Price was good for what you get! Jump on this place now Greenville!!! Thanks Jonny!!! ||| My daughter brought me here because it is her favorite spot in Greenville, and now I can see why!
The food was delicious! We ordered the pot sticker and shrimp spring rolls as an app, and then we moved out to the Pho bowls. I ordered shrimp and my daughter ordered steaks. I mean DELICIOUS, hot, and filling!
Again, I can now see why this place is her favorite! The food and service were top notch! ||| The food was great!! I had tea that was actually freshly steeped! Haven't had that in awhile! We had shrimp pho and bon mi, a sandwich I believe. Everything was delicious and the server gave excellent service! ||| Pretty solid restaurant if you need a quick pho fix. Food was pretty good, not the best, def not the worst. Started off a little rough when I came in to get a table because the guy just said "what you want?" So I just responded by saying "to eat" lol. Told me to sit anywhere and he got me all set with my order. The food came out hot and fast, the only complaint with the food was that the noodles in my pho were like super stuck together and I had to do a bit of work to get them situated. Also the soy sauce was in some weird sprayer so I couldn't tell how much I was putting in it.  I had the eye of round pho because I don't like the texture of the fatty meats and it was good. I also did the rocket shrimp and they were a little tough but not too bad. I would def come back again. ||| +updated+
Another wonderful meal at one of my favorite places in Greenville. I ordered thw same thing as always except seafood Pho today.

I always order grilled beef spring rolls, lemon grass beef vermicelli & house special pho. Everything is always incredible and reasonably priced for the quality.

Fresh ingredients, noodles & incredible service. If you haven't tried this place, you're missing out on one of the best local places in town 💜💛

google_reviews:
I visited this IHOP three times with friends on Thanksgiving Day (2022, 2024 and 2025), and one other time by myself in May 2023. In each visit, I ordered the same thing: quick 2-egg breakfast, with eggs scrambled, 2 sausage links and wheat toast.   I had a different server every time and have not been disappointed. Food and service was consistently good. Price was reasonable. This IHOP is suitable for solo or group party. Parking (free) was plentiful when I was there (between 8am to 11am). I recommend this place to anyone looking for a decent meal - especially the quick 2-egg breakfast - at a reasonable price. ||| It took awhile before someone came to seat us. There were 3 different parties waiting to be seated and employees either just walked by or looked at us from across the restaurant. Mine you, the place was almost empty yet there were several employees there.

After that though, the server was attentive and the food was good. But the first impression was horrible. ||| From the moment we walked into the door,it was an experience that set the right expectations to come. Erin, our server, did not miss. She was courteous and professional. The food was better than we anticipated and the chef provided a meal to remember.  Everyone was friendly and the facility was clean. Thank you to the team at North Pointe! ||| We have come here before. It used to be popping with a line out the door.  To be honest I thought it was closed for a while now because it's always so dead but I was craving IHOP so we came. I got a chicken fried steak and eggs. They were served with a plastic take out fork, no real silverware. The fork melted in the hot steak and cut my mouth and it bent as you tried to cut the food.  We had to request silverware. Our waitress spent more time talking with the only other worker in the restaurant who we never saw (girl ...girl ...girl every 5 minutes so it must have been a juicy combo). It was so dead. We never were asked about refills after the first time when out food came out. We had to request the gravy for the steak because it was just a little dollap on the steak. My husband never got the salsa that was supposed to come with his omelette. The pancakes came on small side plates too small to hold more than the pancake so syrup went everywhere.

I don't know what the hell was going on. It was one cook and one waitress per side of the restaurant and the waitress walked everywhere scuffing her feet like she was wearing slippers at home.  I dunno if it was a bunch of high schoolers or what but during a dinner rush there were 3 parties in the whole place. Just close it down already. The service was left a lot to be desired. How do you do bad service with less than 3 tables?

When we paid the girl walked by smelling like straight skunk and when I remarked about it to my husband on the way to our car he said there had been a blunt roach in the toilet in the bathroom.

We will not be returning and we will be sure to tell everyone why it's so dead here now. ||| Took my daughter here as she had never been to an IHOP.  The waitress was very kind and attentive to her and needs.  She had the kid's meal and it had chocolate so you know she was happy.  I had the breakfast tacos.  Very good, just needed seasoning on my eggs.  Overall, I would come back here again if I was in the area.

Not from the area but will definitely stop back by anytime I am! The pic is of the leftovers. Would've took one before but it was too delicious ||| If you've never been to Mexico or been invited to a homemade lunch by a Mexican construction crew and all you know about "Mexican" food is anglicized tex-mex with cheap bean and dairy fillers, then welcome to Mexico at Los Querreros! Family run culinary geniuses serving a very tasty and varied menu of authentic Mexican food and beverages.Im a regular and always leave happy and content.
